name: Red Snapper in Phyllo
recipeIngredient: 4 small red snapper fillets (about 5 oz each)
1/2 cup unsalted butter (cold), cut into 8 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Melt 6 tablespoons of butter.
3. Place one sheet of phyllo dough on the work surface, short side facing up. Keep remaining dough covered with a damp kitchen towel.
4. Brush the sheet with butter and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon bread crumbs.
5. Top with another sheet of phyllo and brush with butter.
6. Place one fish fillet on the short end of the phyllo, about 1 inch from the end and 3 inches from both sides. If necessary, cut the fillet to fit.
7. Sprinkle fish with 1 teaspoon Pernod and season lightly with salt and pepper.
8. Place 1 tablespoon butter on the fish.
9. Fold the long sides over the fish, then fold the bottom edge up, and finally fold to the top, creating a neat rectangular package.
10. Place the seam side down on the baking sheet. Repeat to make 4 packages.
11. Brush the tops and sides of the packages with remaining butter and sprinkle lightly with paprika.
12. Bake until golden brown (about 30 minutes).
Meanwhile, prepare the sauce:
- Cook shallot in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until softened for about 1 1/2 minutes.
- Reduce heat to medium-low and whisk in butter one piece at a time, adding each piece after the previous is incorporated.
- Continue reducing heat if needed so that the butter does not melt before it emulsifies. Stir in parsley and season with pepper.
To serve: Place hot phyllo packages on serving plates and drizzle each one with warm sauce. Serves 4. Preparation time: 30 minutes. Cooking time: 30 minutes.
